The Boraginaceae family comprises over 2,000 species of plants, including many species of borage, heliotrope, and forget-me-not. Odontarrhena decipiens is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial herb that grows in a variety of habitats, including deserts and mountainous regions.
